steps.title: Translate the condition to an inequality
steps.content: The problem states Tom needs a mean of at least 90 on four exams. With three known scores (85, 78, 98) and an unknown fourth score G, the mean of these four exams is: $$\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4}$$

steps.title: Set up the mean inequality
steps.content: For the mean to be at least 90, we write: $$\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4} \ge 90$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
